inline_comments.message:
running maven build and grepping log output is quite dummy way to detect these.

More correct would be prob. using Maven dependency plugin and output results to some structured file e.g. xml format that can be further parsed with code.

E.g. something like this:
mvn dependency:tree -DoutputFile=file.graphml -DoutputType=graphml

yeah it is dummy, but works. I tested all dependency options, vide jira. None of them works for numerous reasons. The example you have prepared would fail because: internal dependencies (one project depends on other project in the same pom), projects using plugins "execute bash" - my favorite!, projects where dynamic dependencies are used and finally for some reason dependency plugin does not collect all the plugins in all needed versions. My conclusion is:  to build offline first you have to have correct online build
Why? I mean the file should be created if it doesn't exist at later stages. Unless your intention was to fail early if it would fail creation.
